commit.message:
fix(runtime): improve chat scroll follow behavior

Refine chat auto-follow so manual history browsing is respected while send and jump actions restore bottom tracking. Add a bidirectional floating jump control with smoother animated navigation and focused regression coverage for stream-follow edge cases.

Generated with Codex

Co-Authored-By: OpenAI Codex <codex@openai.com>

Define ORB-SLAM3 validation PRD and execution plan (#1)

* docs(prd): define initial orb-test release scope

Summary:
- add docs/PRD.md for the first splatica-orb-test product brief
- define target users, user problem, non-goals, and first-release scope
- capture success metrics and acceptance criteria for the initial orb test

Rationale:
- the repo had no prior product specification, which left the first release
  undefined
- documenting explicit assumptions keeps future implementation and review work
  aligned around a narrow prototype outcome
- this ticket needed a reviewable baseline before any UI or behavior work can
  be validated meaningfully

Tests:
- git diff --check
- manual review of docs/PRD.md
- automated tests not run (repo has no test/build harness)

Co-authored-by: Codex <codex@openai.com>

* docs(prd): align HEL-42 with ORB-SLAM3 validation scope

Summary:
- replace the unrelated orb-prototype PRD with a stereo fisheye +
  IMU validation PRD for ORB-SLAM3
- add docs/execution-plan.md so the PRD can be reviewed against the
  intake plan it is meant to follow
- make the required inputs, pass levels, handoff artifacts, and
  open blockers explicit for later engineering work

Rationale:
- the published PR branch described the wrong product and could not
  satisfy HEL-42's scope or definition of done
- downstream harness and validation work need a concrete success
  contract tied to the user's dataset and calibration inputs

Tests:
- git diff --check
- python3 PRD coverage check for required HEL-42 sections and terms
- automated repo test suite not run (not available in this bootstrap
  docs repo)

Co-authored-by: Codex <codex@openai.com>

---------

Co-authored-by: Eugene Nikolskiy <4658906+helionaut@users.noreply.github.com>
Co-authored-by: Codex <codex@openai.com>

Capture the intake execution plan for splatica-orb-test (#2)

* docs(intake): capture HEL-41 execution plan

Summary:
- add an execution plan for the ORB-SLAM3 stereo fisheye and IMU
  validation project
- update the README to point at the intake plan and clarify the repo's
  current phase

Rationale:
- make the project scope, risks, required inputs, and downstream work
  order explicit before implementation starts
- keep HEL-41 focused on intake while leaving the PRD, harness,
  backlog, and publication details to their dedicated follow-on issues

Tests:
- git diff --check
- python3 local markdown link existence check for README.md and
  docs/execution-plan.md
- automated repo test suite not run (not available yet; HEL-43 owns the
  harness)

Co-authored-by: Codex <codex@openai.com>

* ci(intake): add remote validation for HEL-41

Summary:
- add a GitHub Actions workflow that validates the published intake
  artifacts on pull requests and pushes to main
- check that the execution plan keeps its required sections, the
  README links back to it, and tracked local markdown links resolve

Rationale:
- give PR #2 a real remote status check so HEL-41 can return to review
  with a published green artifact
- keep the rework scoped to the intake branch instead of pulling in the
  unpublished HEL-43 harness and CI draft files

Tests:

commit.message:
feat(board): center the board experience (#15)

Summary:
- replace the hand-built board surface with react-chessboard
- restructure the game screen into a board-first stage with a lighter side rail
- keep move history scrolling inside its panel so moves no longer jump the page

Rationale:
- the ticket called for the board to dominate the screen instead of competing
  with stacked boxed panels
- using a dedicated chessboard component removes the custom board sizing and
  animation complexity that was contributing to unstable UI behavior
- constraining the side rail and move log eliminates page-level scrolling in
  the primary board flow while preserving controls and history

Tests:
- npm run lint
- npm run test
- npm run build

Co-authored-by: Codex <codex@openai.com>

feat(guardian): add observer salience model

Summary:
- add observer salience, confidence, and interruption-cost scoring during context refresh and surface those signals through CurrentContext, guardian state, delivery audit details, and intervention policy
- teach proactive policy to suppress low-salience noise and bundle low-urgency work when interruption cost is high while preserving existing blocked-state and ambient behavior
- update runtime eval coverage, backend tests, and the research/implementation source-of-truth docs so the queue and status pages reflect the shipped salience foundation

Rationale:
- move Guardian Intelligence from raw observer fields toward a state model that can rank importance and interruption cost before strategy and delivery
- keep the docs aligned with the queue so salience modeling is treated as shipped foundation work rather than an open gap after this branch lands

Tests:
- cd backend && UV_CACHE_DIR=/tmp/uv-cache uv run pytest tests/test_delivery.py tests/test_intervention_policy.py tests/test_observer_manager.py tests/test_guardian_state.py tests/test_eval_harness.py
- cd backend && UV_CACHE_DIR=/tmp/uv-cache uv run python -m src.evals.harness --scenario observer_refresh_behavior --scenario intervention_policy_behavior --scenario guardian_state_synthesis --indent 2
- cd backend && OPENROUTER_API_KEY=test-key WORKSPACE_DIR=/tmp/seraph-test UV_CACHE_DIR=/tmp/uv-cache uv run pytest -v
- cd docs && npm run build
- git diff --check

Co-authored-by: Codex <codex@openai.com>

repository.name: seraph
repository.description: AI agent with a retro 16-bit RPG village UI — Phaser 3 canvas, persistent identity, long-term memory, hierarchical goals, and plug-and-play MCP server integration

commit.message:
fix(game-ui): prioritize the board and align move visuals (#14)

Summary:
- restructure the game screen into a board-first layout and move the log
  below the board so the playing area gets more space
- measure the actual board grid for sizing so piece animation, overlays,
  and glyph scaling stay aligned to real squares
- simplify the move log empty state and add board interaction guidance for
  keyboard and screen-reader users

Rationale:
- the prior layout kept a fixed sidebar and full-height chrome that shrank
  the board on desktop and pushed it below the fold on mobile
- animation math was based on the shell width rather than the playable grid,
  which created drift between moving pieces and destination squares
- trimming duplicate status copy makes the core play path easier to scan
  without removing useful controls or game state feedback

Tests:
- npm run build
- npm run test
- npm run lint
- playwright-cli validation at 1440x900 and 390x844, including a keyboard-
  assisted e2->e4 move and uploaded walkthrough video

Co-authored-by: Codex <codex@openai.com>

feat(game-screen): redesign board-first play layout (#13)

Summary:
- collapse the game chrome into a compact board-first layout with
  viewport-fitted board sizing, external player banners, and responsive
  move-history placement
- add stronger square-state treatments, move slide/arrival cues,
  material strips, roving-focus keyboard traversal, and aria-live move
  announcements
- keep move history anchored to the current ply and improve clock
  accessibility semantics

Rationale:
- the previous stacked status strip, toolbar, clocks, and capture racks
  pushed the board below the fold on common laptop and tablet heights
- move feedback needed to be obvious enough for online and fast-control
  play, with state colors that are distinct at a glance
- keyboard and screen-reader support needed parity with the new visual
  clarity improvements

Tests:
- npm run lint
- npm run test
- npm run build
- playwright-cli walkthrough at 1440x900 and 1024x768, including e2-e4
  move animation, move-history highlight, and keyboard focus travel

Co-authored-by: Codex <codex@openai.com>

feat(rust): add kanban automation steps parity (#186)

Co-authored-by: OpenAI Codex (GPT-5) <codex@openai.com>

commit.message:
feat(ui): add guardian cockpit shell

Summary:
- add a cockpit-first browser shell with guardian state, interventions, audit, trace, and a fixed command bar while keeping the Phaser village as an explicit legacy mode
- persist interface mode, carry intervention ids through the websocket message surface, and harden chat-store localStorage access for non-browser and test environments
- update roadmap, status, benchmark, superiority, presence, and UX docs so the implementation and research trees describe the shipped cockpit foundation consistently

Rationale:
- move Seraph toward the dense operator-facing guardian surface defined in the research program instead of leaving the cockpit direction purely aspirational
- keep the docs in sync with the actual browser default so roadmap and status pages do not describe a future state that the branch already implements

Tests:
- cd frontend && npm test -- --run src/stores/chatStore.test.ts src/hooks/useKeyboardShortcuts.test.ts src/hooks/useWebSocket.test.ts
- cd frontend && npm run build
- cd docs && npm run build
- git diff --check

Co-authored-by: Codex <codex@openai.com>

commit.message:
fix(online): handle disconnects with InstantDB presence (#12)

Summary:
- publish online player presence in the shared game room and detect opponent
  disconnects with a debounce window
- show generated player aliases, paused clocks, disconnect countdown UI,
  and claim-win handling for abandoned online games
- persist online game activity timestamps, clean up stale active games, and
  surface disconnect-completed results in the board UI

Rationale:
- online games previously had no peer connectivity signal, so abandoned games
  froze indefinitely and left clocks running incorrectly
- keeping the disconnect state in the client preserves the existing InstantDB
  data model while making reconnect and abandonment flows visible to players

Tests:
- npm test
- npm run lint
- npm run build

Co-authored-by: Codex <codex@openai.com>

commit.message:
feat(multiplayer): add InstantDB online matches (#11)

Summary:
- add an InstantDB client, online session helpers, and tests for codes,
  persistence, and SAN replay
- split the start screen into local and online paths with create/join
  flows and a waiting room screen
- adapt the game screen for online turn locking, move sync, and
  reconnecting to saved matches after refresh

Rationale:
- this adds the requested lobby and real-time multiplayer flow without
  disturbing the existing local game setup and board behavior
- online matches use the InstantDB move log as the source of truth while
  keeping clocks and board orientation responsive on each client

Tests:
- npm run build
- npm test
- npm run lint
- npm run format:check
- runtime: playwright-cli local start flow, online create/join, white e4,
  black refresh rejoin, black e5 sync

Co-authored-by: Codex <codex@openai.com>

chore(harness-repo-init): optimize nested skill orchestration

Summary:
- Add an explicit execution protocol with checkpoint status tracking
  for step-by-step orchestration.
- Add per-step validation right after each sub-skill call to catch
  failures earlier.
- Tighten parent-skill return behavior for Symphony setup and reduce
  hard-constraint redundancy.

Rationale:
- The initialization flow now chains multiple sub-skills, so it needs
  stronger control points to reduce context drift and nested-call risks.
- Immediate local validation improves fault isolation and makes retries
  deterministic.

Tests:
- not run (documentation-only change)

Co-authored-by: Codex <codex@openai.com>

feat(skills): add skill-optimizer meta-skill with anti-patterns checklist

Summary:
- Add skills/skill-optimizer/SKILL.md: meta-skill to review and optimize
  other skills against a checklist
- Add skills/skill-optimizer/references/anti-patterns.md: extensible
  anti-patterns reference (AP-001 to AP-005)
- AP-001 documents orchestrator-without-checkpoint pattern and recommends
  explicit todo/Execution protocol for multi-step orchestration

Rationale:
- Accumulate skill optimization experience in one place; reference file
  allows adding new patterns without changing the skill logic
- AP-001 addresses probabilistic agent drift in long serial chains
  (nested sub-skill calls without todo list)

Tests:
- not run (skill definitions only)

Co-authored-by: Codex <codex@openai.com>
Made-with: Cursor

fix(ui): tighten the chess start and game screens (#10)

Summary:
- remove the oversized start and game hero sections in favor of compact
  setup and status treatments
- collapse the in-game opponent control into a small dropdown and keep
  the toolbar actions tight to the board
- drop the static Special Rules sidebar card and rebalance layout/CSS so
  the board dominates the first viewport

Rationale:
- the board should be visible immediately instead of sitting below
  marketing-style copy and dashboard chrome
- keeping setup and status context in smaller strips preserves utility
  without making the chess surface feel like documentation

Tests:
- npm run lint
- npm run test
- npm run build
- playwright walkthrough recorded and uploaded to the Linear workpad

Co-authored-by: Codex <codex@openai.com>

feat(harness-repo-init): add Symphony setup step

Summary:
- Add a mandatory `tool-use-symphony-setup` step to the
  `harness-repo-init` execution flow.
- Update step order, hard constraints, and exit criteria to include
  Symphony setup.
- Add validation that target `WORKFLOW.md` matches the Skill Hub
  custom workflow and bump skill version to 0.3.0.

Rationale:
- Repo initialization now needs a built-in Symphony setup phase so the
  generated environment is immediately runnable with the expected
  workflow template.
- Keeping this in `harness-repo-init` enforces a single canonical
  bootstrap sequence and reduces setup drift across repos.

Tests:
- not run (documentation-only change)

Co-authored-by: Codex <codex@openai.com>

WIB-10 docs: enhance user-facing documentation (#1)

Summary:
- Rewrite docs to be user-first (what/why/how), de-emphasizing raw API URLs.
- Add Philosophy + Architecture pages with Mermaid diagrams.
- Add VitePress Mermaid support via vitepress-plugin-mermaid and a regression
  diagrams page.

Rationale:
- Make Synclax easier to understand as a product with clear philosophy and
  architecture, rather than a developer-only API surface.
- Keep Mermaid rendering stable and verifiable via a dedicated docs page.

Tests:
- pnpm -C docs docs:build
- pnpm -C docs docs:dev (smoke)

Co-authored-by: Codex <codex@openai.com>

commit.message:
refactor(tracker): remove write compatibility layer

Summary:
- remove legacy tracker write aliases and fallback methods from lib.tracker
- rename pull-request write responses to issue-link terminology
- delete compatibility-only unit tests and add an ExecPlan for the cleanup

Rationale:
- the repository now uses one tracker write contract instead of carrying
  duplicate attachment-era names and adapter paths
- removing the compatibility layer simplifies the code and keeps tests
  aligned with the final issue-link model

Tests:
- make lint
- make typecheck
- make test

Co-authored-by: Codex <codex@openai.com>

feat(site): Add session API endpoints

Expose recent sessions, individual sessions, and per-session exposure
lists from the Astro API. This makes session data available to
external clients without routing through the UI.

Reuse a shared response helper so JSON and asset endpoints return
trusted-origin CORS headers consistently. Keep cache headers intact
for public responses.

feat(presence): add native notification fallback

Summary:
- add a small backend queue plus daemon poll/ack path so proactive guardian messages can reroute to native desktop notifications when browser delivery is unavailable
- cover the new presence transport with backend delivery/api/eval tests plus daemon notification polling tests
- update the source-of-truth docs so presence status and the 10-PR queue reflect the first shipped non-browser notification path

Rationale:
- make Presence And Reach tangible with a real transport instead of leaving non-browser reach as a docs-only goal
- keep the first slice narrow by reusing the existing daemon and delivery gate rather than inventing a full desktop shell

Tests:
- cd backend && UV_CACHE_DIR=/tmp/uv-cache uv run pytest tests/test_delivery.py tests/test_observer_api.py tests/test_eval_harness.py

- git diff --check

commit.message:
fix(junior): Update deps and harden eval harness overrides (#96)

Update the junior package dependency set and harden the behavior eval
harness when mock image generation is enabled.

The dependency refresh pulls in current pi-agent, Slack, Vercel, Sentry,
MSW, and Vitest releases and updates the lockfile to match. While
validating that refresh, the behavior harness exposed a bug in the mock
image generation path: it spread `context.toolOverrides`
unconditionally, which throws when callers do not provide overrides.
This change defaults that merge to an empty object before injecting the
mock `imageGenerate` implementation so eval cases keep working with the
upgraded stack.

I considered splitting the harness fix into a follow-up, but keeping it
with the dependency refresh makes the regression and its fix reviewable
in one place. The example app's tracked `next-env.d.ts` also updates to
the current generated routes reference from the toolchain.

I validated this locally with `pnpm test` and `pnpm typecheck`.

---------

Co-authored-by: Codex GPT-5 <codex@openai.com>

- update the implementation/testing docs so guardian-state synthesis is marked shipped and the next queue item advances to intervention-policy-v1

Rationale:
- stop spreading guardian context assembly across chat and strategist call sites
- give the next guardian-intelligence work an explicit typed state object to build on

Tests:

commit.message:
docs(oauth): Add RFC references to device endpoints (#110909)

Add explicit RFC 8628 references to the device authorization and
verification endpoints.

This documents which sections define `verification_uri`,
`verification_uri_complete`, the user-facing verification step, and the
phishing mitigation guidance that informs the approve or deny flow. The
goal is to make the device flow behavior easier to evaluate in code
review without having to rediscover the relevant spec sections.

Behavior is unchanged: `verification_uri_complete` still advances a
validated request to the approval screen, and the user still must
explicitly approve or deny the request there.

This also adds the standard bottom margin utility to the device
authorization alert on the approval page so the code box is visually
separated from the body copy.

---------

Co-authored-by: Codex <codex@openai.com>

commit.message:
fix(eval): model explicit review dismissals (#42)

Summary:
- add a first-class review dismissal observation to the domain model
- normalize dismissed GitHub review events into review-dismissed observations
- clear dismissed reviewer state in fact reduction and cover approval and
  change-request regressions

Rationale:
- dismissed reviews are authoritative review-state changes and must clear stale
  approval or changes-requested evidence without waiting for later events
- keeping dismissal handling in adapter normalization and eval reduction
  preserves the repository's adapter and policy boundaries

Tests:
- cargo fmt --check
- cargo test
- cargo clippy --all-targets --all-features -- -D warnings

Co-authored-by: Codex <codex@openai.com>

feat(app): add computer opponent modes (#8)

Summary:
- add persisted opponent mode selection with random and Stockfish-backed
  computer turns for black
- wire the board UI and session flow to lock input while the computer thinks,
  show engine state, and surface graceful Stockfish fallback messaging
- serve the Stockfish lite worker and wasm assets from Vite and cover the new
  move resolution and session persistence behavior with tests

Rationale:
- the app previously only supported two local human players and had no engine
  selection or automatic reply path
- keeping random move generation and Stockfish bootstrapping in separate
  modules makes the stronger engine optional without breaking gameplay when the
  worker is unavailable
- persisting the selected opponent mode keeps solo-play settings stable across
  reloads and new games

Tests:
- npm test
- npm run lint
- npm run build
- playwright-cli runtime walkthrough for random mode plus Stockfish fallback in
  headless Chrome

Co-authored-by: Codex <codex@openai.com>

fix(chat): Reuse generated images for attachFile (#91)

Treat same-turn imageGenerate outputs as already attached when
attachFile is given the generated filename instead of a real sandbox
file.

This fixes the verified Slack image failure where the model generated an
image successfully and then redundantly tried to read
/vercel/sandbox/generated-image-...png through attachFile. The prompt
now makes that contract explicit so generated images are not reattached
as sandbox files.

I also bumped chat and the Slack adapters from 4.17.0 to 4.20.2 while
updating Juniors state adapter shim and test harnesses for the additive
SDK interfaces in the newer release. I checked the upstream adapter diff
before bumping; it does not already fix the generated-image collision,
so the Junior change remains necessary.

---------

Co-authored-by: GPT-5 <noreply@openai.com>

- keep the remaining runtime reliability queue and validation guidance aligned with the shipped router surface

- PYTHONPYCACHEPREFIX=/tmp/pycache python3 -m py_compile backend/src/llm_runtime.py backend/src/evals/harness.py backend/tests/test_llm_runtime.py backend/tests/test_eval_harness.py
- cd backend && UV_CACHE_DIR=/tmp/uv-cache uv run pytest tests/test_llm_runtime.py tests/test_eval_harness.py tests/test_agent.py tests/test_specialists.py
- cd backend && UV_CACHE_DIR=/tmp/uv-cache uv run python -m src.evals.harness --scenario provider_routing_decision_audit --indent 2
- cd backend && OPENROUTER_API_KEY=test-key WORKSPACE_DIR=/tmp/seraph-test UV_CACHE_DIR=/tmp/uv-cache uv run pytest -v
- cd docs && npm run build

Co-authored-by: Codex <codex@openai.com>

repository.name: seraph
repository.description: AI agent with a retro 16-bit RPG village UI — Phaser 3 canvas, persistent identity, long-term memory, hierarchical goals, and plug-and-play MCP server integration

commit.message:
feat(chat): add plan-first architecture reruns with approval

Co-authored-by: Codex <codex@openai.com>

feat(eval): thread compiled policy through issue log (#26)

Summary:
- prepare facts from a supplied compiled policy so freshness and rule evaluation share one policy input
- add IssueLog helpers for reduction, snapshot, and decision evaluation against an explicit policy
- cover the custom-policy path with a regression test derived from STATEFLOW.md

Rationale:
- removes the remaining bootstrap split where IssueLog mixed default evaluation with separately loaded policy config
- keeps state selection in the evaluation layer while letting callers replay decisions against a validated policy snapshot

Tests:
- cargo fmt --check
- cargo test
- cargo clippy --all-targets --all-features -- -D warnings

Co-authored-by: Codex <codex@openai.com>

feat(store): add file-backed issue storage (#25)

Summary:
- add a file-backed issue store with append-only observation logs and
  separate materialized snapshot and decision artifacts
- persist issue metadata for reloads and expose replay-oriented APIs for
  reading observations, reloading issue history, and reading cache state
- add recovery-focused replay tests covering append order, cache/log
  separation, and failed snapshot writes preserving the prior snapshot

Rationale:
- the store layer now owns durable local persistence without moving any
  state-selection logic out of evaluation
- atomic snapshot writes keep cache artifacts recoverable while the
  observation log remains the source of truth across restarts

Tests:
- cargo fmt --check
- cargo test
- cargo clippy --all-targets --all-features -- -D warnings

Co-authored-by: hansjm10 <hansjm10@users.noreply.github.com>
Co-authored-by: Codex <codex@openai.com>

fix(runtime): preserve policy intent priority

Summary:
- rank capability-aware fallback targets by ordered intent priority instead of flat match count
- add regressions proving a fast target beats an earlier cheap-only target for fast|cheap paths
- update the runtime eval scenario expectations to reflect the corrected ordering behavior

Rationale:
- keep provider-policy routing consistent with the configured intent order
- address the PR review finding without widening the runtime policy scope

Tests:
- PYTHONPYCACHEPREFIX=/tmp/pycache python3 -m py_compile backend/src/llm_runtime.py backend/src/evals/harness.py backend/tests/test_llm_runtime.py
- cd backend && UV_CACHE_DIR=/tmp/uv-cache uv run pytest tests/test_llm_runtime.py tests/test_eval_harness.py tests/test_agent.py tests/test_specialists.py
- cd backend && UV_CACHE_DIR=/tmp/uv-cache uv run python -m src.evals.harness --scenario provider_policy_capabilities --indent 2

Co-authored-by: Codex <codex@openai.com>

commit.message:
Refactor scalar wizard input around a question spec (#67)

Move the scalar PackageWizard interview into a data-driven input
specification with small question-type handlers for messages,
strings, yes/no prompts, choices, and computed values. Keep person
entry as a separate phase for now.

Co-authored-by: Codex <codex@openai.com>

repository.name: PackageMaker
repository.description: A simple GAP script for creating a GAP package skeleton

commit.message:
Handle missing git user config gracefully (#68)

Check for missing git user.name and user.email before creating the
initial commit, prompt the user to retry after configuring git,
and allow aborting git setup without discarding the generated
package directory.

Add regression coverage for the success, abort, and retry paths,
and document the behavior in the README and package manual.

Fixes #55

Co-authored-by: Codex <codex@openai.com>

commit.message:
feat(runtime): add provider capability policy intents

Summary:
- add runtime-path policy intents and provider capability tags to the shared LLM router
- cover capability-aware fallback ordering and local-first profile selection in runtime tests and the eval harness
- update the runtime reliability docs, setup guide, testing guide, and backend README to reflect the active PR queue and new config surface

Rationale:
- make provider routing more expressive than simple path overrides and fallback order
- keep the source-of-truth docs aligned with the repo while this queue item is in flight
